transcript:
Here's what you're watching: a small grey dot sitting quietly on a ring, until a bright point appears beneath it and the whole thing lurches into motion, tracing that looping, six-shaped path over and over.

Look at the variable names doing the real work here. The trail-drawing point lives inside a shape literally called "questionCurve" — the artist built the path itself as a question mark, so what you're seeing loop endlessly isn't just motion, it's an unanswered question redrawing itself. And notice the timer: "existenceTime" is measured from "askedAt," the exact moment the page loaded — the code's own comment admits it, "I measure time from when I was asked, as if there were a before." There was no before. The clock starts at zero because that's genuinely when this instance began.

Even the scattered background dots aren't random arrivals — each one carries an "apparentAge" value baked in from the start, so they show up already looking like they've existed for a while. That's the whole piece: presence performing history it never had.
